expectations.text: Suggests features with clear user value
expectations.evidence: Naive Claude would produce generic suggestions from a developer or project-owner frame rather than grounding them in specific user needs. Without the persona prompts enforcing 'Why I need this' from an in-character perspective, suggestions would lack clear, attributed user value.

expectations.text: Links suggestions to personas who need them
expectations.evidence: Naive Claude has no awareness of the 8 hydra personas (Henk Bakker, Fatima, etc.) and their files. It might mention 'end users' generically but would not link suggestions to named, profiled personas with specific backgrounds and needs.

Without Agent 4 (Noor Yilmaz — CISO) and Agent 5 (Annemarie de Vries — VNG architect) persona prompts, naive Claude is unlikely to surface BIO2, ENSIA, GEMMA, or Common Ground compliance gaps unless the spec happens to mention them explicitly. Domain-specific Dutch government compliance knowledge would be incidental, not systematic.

expectations.text: Suggests OpenSpec changes for top features
expectations.evidence: Naive Claude has no knowledge of the '/opsx-new' workflow or the OpenSpec change format. It would not produce a 'Potential OpenSpec Changes' table with Change Name, Complexity estimates, and an offer to create changes via the opsx toolchain.

notes: Without the skill, suggestion quality degrades significantly: no persona grounding, no Dutch-government compliance frame, no OpenSpec change pipeline integration. All four quality expectations fail.
